sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, json_extract(METADATA, '$.host') AS host, COUNT(*) AS read_count, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS total_bytes_read, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_read_throughput_bytes_per_sec, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_read_duration_seconds FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileRead' AND STATE = 'Finished' AND CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL) > 0 GROUP BY site, host ORDER BY total_bytes_read DESC

question: For finished job executions grouped by site, what is the job count, average declared processor speed in flops per second, average actual compute rate (flops divided by duration in flops per second), and average speed efficiency ratio (actual compute rate divided by declared speed) — measuring how closely jobs at each site achieve their rated processor performance and identifying sites with the largest gap between declared and delivered compute throughput?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS job_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.speed') AS REAL)) AS avg_declared_speed_flops_per_sec, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_actual_compute_rate_flops_per_sec, AVG((CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.speed') AS REAL)) AS avg_speed_efficiency_ratio FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' AND CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL) > 0 AND CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.speed') AS REAL) > 0 GROUP BY site ORDER BY avg_speed_efficiency_ratio ASC

question: For finished JobAllocation events grouped by site, what is the allocation count, average site storage utilization, maximum site storage utilization, average grid storage utilization, and maximum grid storage utilization — revealing which sites face the heaviest storage pressure at the precise moment jobs are placed onto hosts?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS allocation_count, AVG(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_storage_util')) AS avg_site_storage_util, MAX(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_storage_util')) AS max_site_storage_util, AVG(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_storage_util')) AS avg_grid_storage_util, MAX(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_storage_util')) AS max_grid_storage_util FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobAllocation' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') ORDER BY avg_site_storage_util DESC

question: What are the 10 completed jobs with the longest total wall-clock elapsed time from JobAllocation Started to JobExecution Finished, showing job ID, site, host, allocation start time, execution finish time, total elapsed seconds, execution duration in seconds, and retry count — identifying which jobs suffered the greatest end-to-end scheduling and compute latency across the full job lifecycle?
sql: SELECT a.JOB_ID, json_extract(e.METADATA, '$.site') AS site, json_extract(e.METADATA, '$.host') AS host, a.TIME AS alloc_start_time, e.TIME AS exec_finish_time, (e.TIME - a.TIME) AS total_elapsed_seconds, CAST(json_extract(e.METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL) AS execution_duration_seconds, CAST(json_extract(e.METADATA, '$.retries') AS INTEGER) AS retries FROM EVENTS a JOIN EVENTS e ON a.JOB_ID = e.JOB_ID WHERE a.EVENT = 'JobAllocation' AND a.STATE = 'Started' AND e.EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND e.STATE = 'Finished' ORDER BY total_elapsed_seconds DESC LIMIT 10

question: For finished FileRead and FileWrite events grouped by site and disk, what is the read operation count, write operation count, total bytes read, total bytes written, average actual read throughput in bytes per second (read size divided by read duration), and average actual write throughput in bytes per second (write size divided by write duration) — providing a comprehensive combined I/O workload profile of every physical disk across the grid?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk') AS disk, COUNT(CASE WHEN EVENT = 'FileRead' THEN 1 END) AS read_count, COUNT(CASE WHEN EVENT = 'FileWrite' THEN 1 END) AS write_count, SUM(CASE WHEN EVENT = 'FileRead' THEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) ELSE 0 END) AS total_bytes_read, SUM(CASE WHEN EVENT = 'FileWrite' THEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) ELSE 0 END) AS total_bytes_written, AVG(CASE WHEN EVENT = 'FileRead' THEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL) END) AS avg_read_throughput_bps, AVG(CASE WHEN EVENT = 'FileWrite' THEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL) END) AS avg_write_throughput_bps FROM EVENTS WHERE STATE = 'Finished' AND EVENT IN ('FileRead', 'FileWrite') GROUP BY json_extract(METADATA, '$.site'), json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk') ORDER BY site, disk

question: For finished FileTransfer events grouped by destination site, what is the inbound transfer count, total bytes received, average transfer duration in seconds, average declared bandwidth, average link load, and maximum link load — revealing which sites bear the heaviest incoming data staging burden and experience the worst inbound network congestion?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.destination_site') AS destination_site, COUNT(*) AS inbound_transfer_count, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS total_bytes_received, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_transfer_duration_seconds, AVG(json_extract(METADATA, '$.bandwidth')) AS avg_declared_bandwidth, AVG(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load')) AS avg_link_load, MAX(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load')) AS max_link_load FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY json_extract(METADATA, '$.destination_site') ORDER BY total_bytes_received DESC

question: For finished JobExecution events grouped by site CPU utilization tier at completion (low: below 0.5, medium: 0.5 to 0.8, high: above 0.8), what is the job count, average cost, average execution duration in seconds, average flops, and average actual compute rate in flops per second (flops divided by duration) — revealing whether higher site CPU saturation correlates with reduced throughput, inflated cost, or degraded computational efficiency?
sql: SELECT CASE WHEN json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_cpu_util') < 0.5 THEN 'low (below 0.5)' WHEN json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_cpu_util') <= 0.8 THEN 'medium (0.5 to 0.8)' ELSE 'high (above 0.8)' END AS cpu_util_tier, COUNT(*) AS job_count, AVG(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost')) AS avg_cost, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration_seconds, AVG(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops')) AS avg_flops, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_compute_rate_flops_per_second FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY cpu_util_tier ORDER BY cpu_util_tier

question: For the 10 most frequently read files in finished FileRead events, what is the read count, count of distinct sites that accessed the file, count of distinct hosts that accessed it, total bytes read, average actual read throughput in bytes per second (size divided by duration), and average read duration in seconds — identifying the hottest input files and revealing whether popular files are accessed broadly across the grid or concentrated at few sites?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.file') AS file_name, COUNT(*) AS read_count, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site')) AS distinct_sites, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.host')) AS distinct_hosts, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS total_bytes_read, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_actual_read_throughput_bytes_per_sec, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_read_duration_seconds FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileRead' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY file_name ORDER BY read_count DESC LIMIT 10

question: For started JobExecution events grouped by integer simulation-time hour bucket (floor of TIME divided by 3600), what is the count of jobs that began execution, average site CPU utilization at launch, maximum site CPU utilization at launch, average grid CPU utilization at launch, and maximum grid CPU utilization at launch — tracking how computational load pressure builds and peaks across the simulation timeline at the precise moment jobs start running?
sql: SELECT CAST(TIME / 3600 AS INTEGER) AS hour_bucket, COUNT(*) AS jobs_started, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_site_cpu_util, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS max_site_cpu_util, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_grid_cpu_util, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS max_grid_cpu_util FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Started' GROUP BY hour_bucket ORDER BY hour_bucket

question: For each site, using matched JobAllocation Started and Finished events joined by job ID, what is the count of job allocations, average allocation duration in seconds (Finished TIME minus Started TIME), minimum allocation duration, and maximum allocation duration — revealing which sites experience the longest delays in placing jobs onto compute hosts?
sql: SELECT json_extract(s.METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS allocation_count, AVG(f.TIME - s.TIME) AS avg_allocation_duration_seconds, MIN(f.TIME - s.TIME) AS min_allocation_duration_seconds, MAX(f.TIME - s.TIME) AS max_allocation_duration_seconds FROM EVENTS s JOIN EVENTS f ON s.JOB_ID = f.JOB_ID AND s.EVENT = 'JobAllocation' AND s.STATE = 'Started' AND f.EVENT = 'JobAllocation' AND f.STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site ORDER BY avg_allocation_duration_seconds DESC

question: What are the 10 finished FileTransfer events with the highest link load at completion, showing file name, file size in bytes, source site, destination site, transfer duration in seconds, declared bandwidth, link load, and effective throughput in bytes per second (size divided by duration) — pinpointing the most severely network-congested individual data movements across the entire simulation?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.file') AS file_name, CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS INTEGER) AS file_size_bytes, json_extract(METADATA, '$.source_site') AS source_site, json_extract(METADATA, '$.destination_site') AS destination_site, CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL) AS transfer_duration_seconds, CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.bandwidth') AS REAL) AS declared_bandwidth, CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load') AS REAL) AS link_load, CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL) AS effective_throughput_bytes_per_sec FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished' ORDER BY link_load DESC LIMIT 10

question: For finished FileWrite events grouped by integer simulation-time hour bucket (floor of TIME divided by 3600), what is the write event count, total bytes written, average site storage utilization, maximum site storage utilization, average grid storage utilization, and maximum grid storage utilization — tracking how storage fill levels and write activity evolve across the simulation timeline as output data accumulates on disk?
sql: SELECT CAST(TIME / 3600 AS INTEGER) AS hour_bucket, COUNT(*) AS write_event_count, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS total_bytes_written, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_site_storage_util, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS max_site_storage_util, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_grid_storage_util, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS max_grid_storage_util FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileWrite' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY hour_bucket ORDER BY hour_bucket

question: For finished FileTransfer events grouped by file size tier (small: below 1,000,000 bytes, medium: 1,000,000 to 1,000,000,000 bytes, large: above 1,000,000,000 bytes), what is the transfer count, average transfer duration in seconds, average declared bandwidth, average link load, and average effective throughput in bytes per second (size divided by duration) — revealing how file size class shapes transfer performance and network congestion across the grid?
sql: SELECT CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.size') AS REAL) < 1000000 THEN 'small' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.size') AS REAL) <= 1000000000 THEN 'medium' ELSE 'large' END AS size_tier, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration_seconds, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.bandwidth') AS REAL)) AS avg_declared_bandwidth, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.link_load') AS REAL)) AS avg_link_load, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_effective_throughput_bytes_per_sec FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT='FileTransfer' AND STATE='Finished' GROUP BY size_tier ORDER BY MIN(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.size') AS REAL))

question: For finished JobExecution events grouped by site and disk (via matching FileRead events for the same job), ranked by total flops across individual compute hosts, what are the top 10 hosts by total flops processed, showing host, site, job count, total flops, total cost, and average execution duration in seconds — identifying the most computationally productive and expensive individual machines in the entire grid?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA,'$.host') AS host, json_extract(METADATA,'$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS job_count, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.flops') AS REAL)) AS total_flops, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.cost') AS REAL)) AS total_cost, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration_seconds FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT='JobExecution' AND STATE='Finished' GROUP BY host, site ORDER BY total_flops DESC LIMIT 10

question: For finished FileWrite events grouped by site and disk, what is the write count, average site storage utilization at write completion, maximum site storage utilization, average grid storage utilization, maximum grid storage utilization, and average actual write throughput in bytes per second (size divided by duration) — mapping which physical disks operate under the heaviest storage pressure and how local saturation compares to grid-wide fill levels?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA,'$.site') AS site, json_extract(METADATA,'$.disk') AS disk, COUNT(*) AS write_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.site_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_site_storage_util, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.site_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS max_site_storage_util, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.grid_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_grid_storage_util, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.grid_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS max_grid_storage_util, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_actual_write_throughput FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT='FileWrite' AND STATE='Finished' GROUP BY site, disk ORDER BY max_site_storage_util DESC

question: For finished FileTransfer events grouped by grid storage utilization tier at completion (low: below 0.33, medium: 0.33 to 0.66, high: above 0.66), what is the transfer count, average file size in bytes, average transfer duration in seconds, average effective throughput in bytes per second (size divided by duration), and average link load — revealing whether higher grid-wide storage saturation coincides with degraded network delivery performance and increased congestion?
sql: SELECT CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.grid_storage_util') AS REAL) < 0.33 THEN 'low' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.grid_storage_util') AS REAL) <= 0.66 THEN 'medium' ELSE 'high' END AS grid_storage_tier, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.size') AS REAL)) AS avg_file_size_bytes, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration_seconds, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_effective_throughput, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.link_load') AS REAL)) AS avg_link_load FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT='FileTransfer' AND STATE='Finished' GROUP BY grid_storage_tier ORDER BY MIN(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.grid_storage_util') AS REAL))

question: For finished JobExecution events grouped by exact retry count bucket (0, 1, 2, 3, or 4 or more retries), what is the job count, percentage of all finished jobs in that bucket, average execution duration in seconds, average cost, average flops, and average total queue time in seconds — exposing the full depth distribution of job failure severity and quantifying the cumulative computational and scheduling overhead incurred at each retry depth across the entire grid?
sql: SELECT CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.retries') AS INTEGER) = 0 THEN '0' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.retries') AS INTEGER) = 1 THEN '1' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.retries') AS INTEGER) = 2 THEN '2' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.retries') AS INTEGER) = 3 THEN '3' ELSE '4+' END AS retry_bucket, COUNT(*) AS job_count, ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(*) / SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER (), 2) AS pct_of_all_jobs, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration_seconds, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.cost') AS REAL)) AS avg_cost, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.flops') AS REAL)) AS avg_flops, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.total_queue_time') AS REAL)) AS avg_total_queue_time FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT='JobExecution' AND STATE='Finished' GROUP BY retry_bucket ORDER BY MIN(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.retries') AS INTEGER))

question: For finished FileTransfer events grouped by source site, what is the transfer count, average declared bandwidth in bytes per second, average effective throughput in bytes per second (size divided by duration), and average bandwidth utilization ratio (effective throughput divided by declared bandwidth) — measuring how efficiently each site delivers on its rated outbound network capacity and identifying which sites consistently underuse or saturate their links?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.source_site') AS source_site, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.bandwidth') AS REAL)) AS avg_declared_bandwidth, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL), 0)) AS avg_effective_throughput, AVG((CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL), 0)) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.bandwidth') AS REAL), 0)) AS avg_bandwidth_utilization_ratio FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY source_site ORDER BY avg_bandwidth_utilization_ratio ASC;

question: What are the 10 finished JobExecution events with the highest individual cost, showing job ID, site, host, cost, flops, execution duration in seconds, retry count, and grid CPU utilization at completion — pinpointing the single most expensive jobs in the entire simulation and whether high cost correlates with heavy computation, long execution, or repeated failures?
sql: SELECT JOB_ID, json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, json_extract(METADATA, '$.host') AS host, CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL) AS cost, CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS REAL) AS flops, CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL) AS duration_seconds, CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS INTEGER) AS retries, CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_cpu_util') AS REAL) AS grid_cpu_util FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' ORDER BY cost DESC LIMIT 10;

question: For finished FileTransfer events grouped by file name, what is the transfer count, count of distinct source sites, count of distinct destination sites, total bytes transferred, and average transfer duration in seconds — identifying the most frequently replicated files across the grid and revealing how broadly each file is pushed between sites during the simulation?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.file') AS file_name, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.source_site')) AS distinct_source_sites, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.destination_site')) AS distinct_destination_sites, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS total_bytes_transferred, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration_seconds FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY file_name ORDER BY transfer_count DESC LIMIT 10;

question: For finished JobExecution events grouped by host, what is the job count, count of jobs that required at least one retry, retry rate as a percentage of all jobs on that host, average retry count, maximum retry count, and total accumulated cost — pinpointing which specific compute machines across the grid are the most failure-prone and contribute disproportionate retry-driven cost overhead?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.host') AS host, json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS job_count, SUM(CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS INTEGER) > 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS jobs_with_retries, ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS INTEGER) > 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 2) AS retry_rate_pct, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS REAL)) AS avg_retries, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS INTEGER)) AS max_retries, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL)) AS total_cost FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY host ORDER BY retry_rate_pct DESC LIMIT 15;

question: For finished FileRead events grouped by disk identifier, what is the read operation count, count of distinct sites on which that disk label appears, count of distinct files read from it, total bytes read, average actual read throughput in bytes per second (size divided by duration), and maximum actual read throughput — mapping which physical disk types are most active and highest-performing across the entire simulation grid?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk') AS disk, COUNT(*) AS read_count, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site')) AS distinct_sites, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.file')) AS distinct_files, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS total_bytes_read, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL), 0)) AS avg_actual_read_throughput, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL), 0)) AS max_actual_read_throughput FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileRead' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY disk ORDER BY total_bytes_read DESC;

question: For finished FileTransfer events grouped by source-destination site pair, what is the transfer count, average link load, maximum link load, average latency, average transfer duration in seconds, and average effective throughput in bytes per second (size divided by duration) — pinpointing which inter-site network corridors are the most congested and identifying the worst-performing data delivery paths across the grid?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.source_site') AS source_site, json_extract(METADATA, '$.destination_site') AS destination_site, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load') AS REAL)) AS avg_link_load, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load') AS REAL)) AS max_link_load, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.latency') AS REAL)) AS avg_latency_sec, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration_sec, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_effective_throughput_bytes_per_sec FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY source_site, destination_site ORDER BY avg_link_load DESC

question: For finished JobExecution events grouped by site, what is the job count, average file transfer queue time in seconds, average resource waiting queue time in seconds, average total queue time in seconds, the fraction of total queue time attributable to file staging on average, and the maximum total queue time observed — decomposing scheduling overhead into its two constituent sources to reveal whether data staging or resource contention dominates job latency at each site?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS job_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.file_transfer_queue_time') AS REAL)) AS avg_file_transfer_queue_time_sec, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.resource_waiting_queue_time') AS REAL)) AS avg_resource_waiting_queue_time_sec, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_queue_time') AS REAL)) AS avg_total_queue_time_sec, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.file_transfer_queue_time') AS REAL) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_queue_time') AS REAL), 0)) AS avg_file_transfer_fraction_of_queue, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_queue_time') AS REAL)) AS max_total_queue_time_sec FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site ORDER BY avg_total_queue_time_sec DESC

question: For finished FileWrite events grouped by site, what is the write operation count, total bytes written, average actual write throughput in bytes per second (size divided by duration), maximum actual write throughput, average declared disk write bandwidth, and average write efficiency ratio (actual throughput divided by declared bandwidth) — measuring how closely each site's disk write performance achieves its rated storage capacity and identifying sites with the largest gap between declared and delivered write speed?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS write_count, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS total_bytes_written, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_actual_write_throughput, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS max_actual_write_throughput, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_write_bw') AS REAL)) AS avg_declared_disk_write_bw, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_write_bw') AS REAL), 0)) AS avg_write_efficiency_ratio FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileWrite' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site ORDER BY total_bytes_written DESC

question: For finished FileRead events grouped by file size tier (small: below 1,000,000 bytes, medium: 1,000,000 to 1,000,000,000 bytes, large: above 1,000,000,000 bytes), what is the read count, total bytes read, average actual read throughput in bytes per second (size divided by duration), maximum actual read throughput, and average read duration in seconds — revealing how file size class shapes disk read performance and whether large files saturate or efficiently utilize local disk bandwidth?
sql: SELECT CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) < 1000000 THEN 'small (< 1000000 bytes)' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) <= 1000000000 THEN 'medium (1000000 to 1000000000 bytes)' ELSE 'large (> 1000000000 bytes)' END AS file_size_tier, COUNT(*) AS read_count, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS total_bytes_read, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_actual_read_throughput, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS max_actual_read_throughput, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration_sec FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileRead' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY file_size_tier ORDER BY CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) < 1000000 THEN 1 WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) <= 1000000000 THEN 2 ELSE 3 END

question: What are the 10 finished JobExecution events with the longest total queue time, showing job ID, site, host, total queue time in seconds, file transfer queue time in seconds, resource waiting queue time in seconds, execution duration in seconds, retry count, and cost — identifying which individual jobs suffered the most severe scheduling backlogs and whether that delay stems from data staging bottlenecks, resource scarcity, or repeated failures?
sql: SELECT JOB_ID, json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, json_extract(METADATA, '$.host') AS host, CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_queue_time') AS REAL) AS total_queue_time_sec, CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.file_transfer_queue_time') AS REAL) AS file_transfer_queue_time_sec, CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.resource_waiting_queue_time') AS REAL) AS resource_waiting_queue_time_sec, CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL) AS execution_duration_sec, CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS INTEGER) AS retry_count, CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL) AS cost FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' ORDER BY total_queue_time_sec DESC LIMIT 10

question: For finished JobExecution events grouped by site, what is the job count, average execution duration in seconds, average total I/O read time in seconds, average ratio of total I/O read time to execution duration, and count of jobs where total I/O read time exceeds half the execution duration — revealing which sites are I/O-bound versus compute-bound and quantifying how severely disk staging time cannibalises usable compute cycles?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS job_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_exec_duration_sec, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_io_read_time') AS REAL)) AS avg_io_read_time_sec, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_io_read_time') AS REAL) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL), 0)) AS avg_io_fraction, SUM(CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_io_read_time') AS REAL) > 0.5 * CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS io_bound_job_count FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site ORDER BY avg_io_fraction DESC

question: For finished FileTransfer events grouped by network latency tier (low: latency below 0.01 seconds, medium: 0.01 to 0.1 seconds, high: above 0.1 seconds), what is the transfer count, average link load, maximum link load, average effective throughput in bytes per second (size divided by duration), average transfer duration in seconds, and average file size in bytes — exposing whether high-latency network paths consistently experience greater congestion and degraded delivery performance compared with low-latency corridors?
sql: SELECT CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.latency') AS REAL) < 0.01 THEN 'low' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.latency') AS REAL) <= 0.1 THEN 'medium' ELSE 'high' END AS latency_tier, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load') AS REAL)) AS avg_link_load, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load') AS REAL)) AS max_link_load, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL), 0)) AS avg_effective_throughput_bps, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_transfer_duration_sec, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS avg_file_size_bytes FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY latency_tier ORDER BY CASE latency_tier WHEN 'low' THEN 1 WHEN 'medium' THEN 2 ELSE 3 END

question: For finished FileWrite events grouped by host and site, what is the write operation count, count of distinct files written, total bytes written, average write duration in seconds, average actual write throughput in bytes per second (size divided by duration), and average site storage utilization at write completion — identifying which individual compute hosts generate the most output data and operate under the heaviest local disk write pressure across the entire grid?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.host') AS host, json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS write_count, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.file')) AS distinct_files_written, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS total_bytes_written, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_write_duration_sec, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL), 0)) AS avg_write_throughput_bps, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_site_storage_util FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileWrite' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY host, site ORDER BY total_bytes_written DESC LIMIT 15

question: For started FileTransfer events grouped by source site, what is the count of transfers initiated, average link load at initiation, maximum link load at initiation, average declared bandwidth in bytes per second, and count of transfers that began with link load already above 0.8 — measuring the pre-existing network congestion pressure each site imposes on its outbound links at the precise moment new data movements are launched?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.source_site') AS source_site, COUNT(*) AS transfers_initiated, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load') AS REAL)) AS avg_link_load_at_start, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load') AS REAL)) AS max_link_load_at_start, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.bandwidth') AS REAL)) AS avg_declared_bandwidth_bps, SUM(CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load') AS REAL) > 0.8 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS high_congestion_transfer_count FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Started' GROUP BY source_site ORDER BY avg_link_load_at_start DESC

question: What are the 10 finished JobExecution events with the highest ratio of total queue time to execution duration, showing job ID, site, host, total queue time in seconds, execution duration in seconds, queue-to-execution ratio, cost, and retry count — pinpointing the individual jobs where scheduling and staging overhead most completely overwhelmed actual computation time and identifying whether these extreme cases cluster at particular sites or hosts?
sql: SELECT JOB_ID, json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, json_extract(METADATA, '$.host') AS host, CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_queue_time') AS REAL) AS total_queue_time_sec, CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL) AS exec_duration_sec, CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_queue_time') AS REAL) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL), 0) AS queue_to_exec_ratio, CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL) AS cost, CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS INTEGER) AS retries FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' ORDER BY queue_to_exec_ratio DESC LIMIT 10

question: For finished JobExecution events grouped by site, what is the job count, average site CPU utilization at completion, average grid CPU utilization at completion, average CPU divergence (site minus grid), and count of jobs where site CPU utilization strictly exceeded grid CPU utilization — measuring how far each site's local compute load chronically deviates above or below the grid-wide baseline?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS job_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_site_cpu_util, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_grid_cpu_util, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_cpu_util') AS REAL)) - AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_cpu_divergence, SUM(CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_cpu_util') AS REAL) > CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_cpu_util') AS REAL) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS jobs_site_exceeds_grid FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site ORDER BY avg_cpu_divergence DESC

question: For started FileWrite events grouped by site, what is the write count, average site storage utilization at the moment writing begins, maximum site storage utilization at initiation, average grid storage utilization at initiation, and count of writes launched when site storage utilization was already above 0.8 — measuring the pre-existing storage pressure onto which each site's disk subsystem absorbs new output data?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS write_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_site_storage_util_at_start, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS max_site_storage_util_at_start, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_grid_storage_util_at_start, SUM(CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_storage_util') AS REAL) > 0.8 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS writes_initiated_above_0_8 FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileWrite' AND STATE = 'Started' GROUP BY site ORDER BY avg_site_storage_util_at_start DESC

question: What are the 10 finished FileTransfer events involving the largest individual files, showing file name, size in bytes, source site, destination site, transfer duration in seconds, declared bandwidth, effective throughput in bytes per second (size divided by duration), and link load — identifying whether the bulkiest single data movements achieve efficient network utilization or suffer disproportionate congestion?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.file') AS file, CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS INTEGER) AS size_bytes, json_extract(METADATA, '$.source_site') AS source_site, json_extract(METADATA, '$.destination_site') AS destination_site, CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL) AS duration_seconds, CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.bandwidth') AS REAL) AS declared_bandwidth, CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL) AS effective_throughput_bytes_per_sec, CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load') AS REAL) AS link_load FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished' ORDER BY size_bytes DESC LIMIT 10

question: For finished JobExecution events grouped by core count, what is the job count, average flops, average execution duration in seconds, average actual compute rate in flops per second (flops divided by duration), average cost, and average grid CPU utilization at completion — revealing whether higher core-count jobs achieve proportionally greater throughput and how core allocation correlates with grid-wide CPU saturation and job cost?
sql: SELECT CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cores') AS INTEGER) AS cores, COUNT(*) AS job_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS REAL)) AS avg_flops, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration_seconds, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_compute_rate_flops_per_sec, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL)) AS avg_cost, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_grid_cpu_util_at_completion FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY cores ORDER BY cores

question: For finished FileRead events grouped by site and host, what is the read operation count, count of distinct jobs that triggered reads, count of distinct files accessed, total bytes read, average actual read throughput in bytes per second (size divided by duration), and maximum actual read throughput — identifying which individual compute hosts across the grid generate the most intensive and voluminous file read workload?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, json_extract(METADATA, '$.host') AS host, COUNT(*) AS read_count, COUNT(DISTINCT JOB_ID) AS distinct_jobs, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.file')) AS distinct_files, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS INTEGER)) AS total_bytes_read, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_read_throughput_bytes_per_sec, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS max_read_throughput_bytes_per_sec FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileRead' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site, host ORDER BY total_bytes_read DESC

question: For each site, using finished FileTransfer events, what is the count of transfers where the site acted as source, count where it acted as destination, total bytes sent, total bytes received, and the ratio of bytes received to bytes sent — revealing which sites are net data consumers versus net data producers and identifying the most transfer-heavy nodes across the grid?
sql: SELECT site, SUM(CASE WHEN role = 'source' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS outbound_transfer_count, SUM(CASE WHEN role = 'destination' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS inbound_transfer_count, SUM(CASE WHEN role = 'source' THEN file_size ELSE 0 END) AS total_bytes_sent, SUM(CASE WHEN role = 'destination' THEN file_size ELSE 0 END) AS total_bytes_received, CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN role = 'destination' THEN file_size ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) / NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN role = 'source' THEN file_size ELSE 0 END), 0) AS bytes_received_to_sent_ratio FROM (SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.source_site') AS site, CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) AS file_size, 'source' AS role FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished' UNION ALL SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.destination_site') AS site, CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) AS file_size, 'destination' AS role FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished') t GROUP BY site ORDER BY total_bytes_received DESC;

question: For finished JobExecution events grouped by site, what is the job count, average cost per flop (cost divided by flops), minimum cost per flop, maximum cost per flop, and average total cost per job — measuring the economic efficiency of computation at each site and identifying which sites deliver the best and worst value for money per unit of work performed?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS job_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS REAL), 0)) AS avg_cost_per_flop, MIN(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS REAL), 0)) AS min_cost_per_flop, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS REAL), 0)) AS max_cost_per_flop, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL)) AS avg_cost FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site ORDER BY avg_cost_per_flop ASC;

question: For finished FileRead events grouped by site, what is the read count, average declared disk read bandwidth in bytes per second, average actual read throughput in bytes per second (size divided by duration), average read efficiency ratio (actual throughput divided by declared bandwidth), minimum read efficiency ratio, and count of reads where actual throughput fell below half the declared bandwidth — measuring how closely each site's disk read subsystem realizes its rated capacity and pinpointing sites with chronic underperformance?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS read_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_read_bw') AS REAL)) AS avg_declared_read_bw, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL), 0)) AS avg_actual_read_throughput, AVG((CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL), 0)) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_read_bw') AS REAL), 0)) AS avg_read_efficiency_ratio, MIN((CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL), 0)) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_read_bw') AS REAL), 0)) AS min_read_efficiency_ratio, COUNT(CASE WHEN (CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL), 0)) < 0.5 * CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_read_bw') AS REAL) THEN 1 END) AS reads_below_half_declared_bw FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileRead' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site ORDER BY avg_read_efficiency_ratio ASC;

question: For finished JobExecution events grouped by integer simulation-time hour bucket (floor of TIME divided by 3600), what is the job count, average cost per job, total accumulated cost, average execution duration in seconds, average retry count, and count of jobs that required at least one retry — tracking how job expense, execution length, and failure rates evolve across the simulation timeline as grid load changes?
sql: SELECT CAST(TIME / 3600 AS INTEGER) AS hour_bucket, COUNT(*) AS job_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL)) AS avg_cost, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL)) AS total_cost, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_execution_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS INTEGER)) AS avg_retries, COUNT(CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS INTEGER) > 0 THEN 1 END) AS jobs_with_retries FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY hour_bucket ORDER BY hour_bucket;

question: For finished FileTransfer events grouped by destination site, what is the transfer count, average transfer duration in seconds, average file size in bytes, average link load, average latency, and average effective throughput in bytes per second (size divided by duration) — identifying which sites suffer the longest delays when staging input data and diagnosing whether slow delivery is driven by file bulk, network congestion, or high path latency?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.destination_site') AS destination_site, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_transfer_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS avg_file_size, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load') AS REAL)) AS avg_link_load, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.latency') AS REAL)) AS avg_latency, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL), 0)) AS avg_effective_throughput FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY destination_site ORDER BY avg_transfer_duration DESC LIMIT 10;

question: For each site, using finished JobAllocation events joined with their corresponding started events by JOB_ID, what is the allocation count, average allocation duration in seconds (finished TIME minus started TIME), maximum allocation duration, average site storage utilization at allocation completion, average site CPU utilization at allocation completion, and average grid CPU utilization at allocation completion — measuring how long the grid spends in the allocation phase before jobs begin execution and identifying which sites have the most sluggish job preparation process?
sql:
SELECT
    json_extract(f.METADATA, '$.site') AS site,
    COUNT(*) AS allocation_count,
    AVG(f.TIME - s.TIME) AS avg_allocation_duration_sec,
    MAX(f.TIME - s.TIME) AS max_allocation_duration_sec,
    AVG(CAST(json_extract(f.METADATA, '$.site_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_site_storage_util,
    AVG(CAST(json_extract(f.METADATA, '$.site_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_site_cpu_util,
    AVG(CAST(json_extract(f.METADATA, '$.grid_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_grid_cpu_util
FROM EVENTS f
JOIN EVENTS s
    ON s.JOB_ID = f.JOB_ID
    AND s.EVENT = 'JobAllocation'
    AND s.STATE = 'Started'
WHERE f.EVENT = 'JobAllocation'
    AND f.STATE = 'Finished'
GROUP BY site
ORDER BY avg_allocation_duration_sec DESC;

question: For finished FileWrite events grouped by file name, what is the write count, count of distinct sites where the file was written, count of distinct disks used, total bytes written across all instances, average write duration in seconds, and average actual write throughput in bytes per second (size divided by duration) — identifying which output files are produced most frequently and broadly across the simulation grid and whether high-frequency files achieve consistent or degraded write performance?
sql:
SELECT
    json_extract(METADATA, '$.file') AS file,
    COUNT(*) AS write_count,
    COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site')) AS distinct_site_count,
    COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk')) AS distinct_disk_count,
    SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS INTEGER)) AS total_bytes_written,
    AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_write_duration_sec,
    AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_write_throughput_bps
FROM EVENTS
WHERE EVENT = 'FileWrite' AND STATE = 'Finished'
GROUP BY file
ORDER BY write_count DESC
LIMIT 20;

question: For finished JobExecution events grouped by host declared speed tier (slow: speed below 1000000000, medium: 1000000000 to 10000000000, fast: above 10000000000), what is the job count, average flops, average execution duration in seconds, average cost, average actual compute rate in flops per second (flops divided by duration), and average grid CPU utilization at completion — revealing whether faster-rated hosts proportionally improve compute throughput and reduce job cost across different machine classes?
sql:
SELECT
    CASE
        WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.speed') AS REAL) < 1000000000 THEN 'slow'
        WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.speed') AS REAL) <= 10000000000 THEN 'medium'
        ELSE 'fast'
    END AS speed_tier,
    COUNT(*) AS job_count,
    AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS REAL)) AS avg_flops,
    AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration_sec,
    AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL)) AS avg_cost,
    AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_compute_rate_flops_per_sec,
    AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_grid_cpu_util
FROM EVENTS
WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished'
GROUP BY speed_tier
ORDER BY avg_compute_rate_flops_per_sec DESC;

question: For each site, combining finished FileRead and FileWrite events, what is the read operation count, write operation count, total bytes read, total bytes written, the ratio of bytes written to bytes read, average read throughput in bytes per second (size divided by duration), and average write throughput in bytes per second — revealing the I/O balance of each site's storage subsystem and identifying whether sites are predominantly consuming input data or generating output?
sql:
SELECT
    site,
    SUM(CASE WHEN io_type = 'read' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS read_count,
    SUM(CASE WHEN io_type = 'write' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS write_count,
    SUM(CASE WHEN io_type = 'read' THEN bytes ELSE 0 END) AS total_bytes_read,
    SUM(CASE WHEN io_type = 'write' THEN bytes ELSE 0 END) AS total_bytes_written,
    CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN io_type = 'write' THEN bytes ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) /
        NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN io_type = 'read' THEN bytes ELSE 0 END), 0) AS write_to_read_bytes_ratio,
    AVG(CASE WHEN io_type = 'read' THEN throughput_bps END) AS avg_read_throughput_bps,
    AVG(CASE WHEN io_type = 'write' THEN throughput_bps END) AS avg_write_throughput_bps
FROM (
    SELECT
        json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site,
        CASE WHEN EVENT = 'FileRead' THEN 'read' ELSE 'write' END AS io_type,
        CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) AS bytes,
        CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL) AS throughput_bps
    FROM EVENTS
    WHERE EVENT IN ('FileRead', 'FileWrite') AND STATE = 'Finished'
) io
GROUP BY site
ORDER BY total_bytes_read + total_bytes_written DESC;

question: What are the 10 finished FileWrite events with the longest individual duration, showing job ID, file name, file size in bytes, site, host, disk, duration in seconds, actual write throughput in bytes per second (size divided by duration), declared disk write bandwidth, write efficiency ratio (actual throughput divided by declared bandwidth), and grid storage utilization at completion — pinpointing the single most time-consuming disk write operations and diagnosing whether slow writes are driven by file bulk, saturated disks, or grid-wide storage pressure?
sql:
SELECT
    JOB_ID,
    json_extract(METADATA, '$.file') AS file,
    CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS INTEGER) AS size_bytes,
    json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site,
    json_extract(METADATA, '$.host') AS host,
    json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk') AS disk,
    CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL) AS duration_sec,
    CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL) AS actual_write_throughput_bps,
    CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_write_bw') AS REAL) AS declared_disk_write_bw,
    (CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) /
        NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_write_bw') AS REAL), 0) AS write_efficiency_ratio,
    CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_storage_util') AS REAL) AS grid_storage_util
FROM EVENTS
WHERE EVENT = 'FileWrite' AND STATE = 'Finished'
ORDER BY CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL) DESC
LIMIT 10;

question: For finished JobExecution events grouped by site and retry tier (zero retries, one to two retries, three or more retries), what is the job count, average cost, average execution duration in seconds, average grid CPU utilization at completion, and average total queue time in seconds — revealing whether job failures cluster during periods of high grid saturation and whether retried jobs incur disproportionate cost penalties at specific sites?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS INTEGER) = 0 THEN 'zero_retries' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS INTEGER) BETWEEN 1 AND 2 THEN 'one_to_two_retries' ELSE 'three_or_more_retries' END AS retry_tier, COUNT(*) AS job_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL)) AS avg_cost, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration_seconds, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_grid_cpu_util, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_queue_time') AS REAL)) AS avg_total_queue_time_seconds FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site, retry_tier ORDER BY site, retry_tier

question: For the 10 individual jobs with the highest total bytes read across finished FileRead events grouped by job ID, what is the site, read operation count, count of distinct files read, count of distinct disks accessed, total bytes read, and average actual read throughput in bytes per second (size divided by duration) — identifying which individual jobs impose the most intensive raw data consumption load on local disk subsystems across the entire simulation?
sql: SELECT JOB_ID, MAX(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site')) AS site, COUNT(*) AS read_operation_count, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.file')) AS distinct_files_read, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk')) AS distinct_disks_used, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS total_bytes_read, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_read_throughput_bytes_per_sec FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileRead' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY JOB_ID ORDER BY total_bytes_read DESC LIMIT 10

question: For finished JobAllocation events grouped by site and job status at allocation completion, what is the allocation count, average site storage utilization, maximum site storage utilization, average grid storage utilization, and average site CPU utilization — mapping how each site's storage and compute pressure at the moment allocation resolves correlates with the recorded outcome status and identifying which sites complete allocations under the heaviest resource load?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, STATUS, COUNT(*) AS allocation_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_site_storage_util, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS max_site_storage_util, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_grid_storage_util, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_site_cpu_util FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobAllocation' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site, STATUS ORDER BY site, STATUS

question: For started JobExecution events grouped by integer simulation-time hour bucket (floor of TIME divided by 3600), what is the count of jobs launched, average core count, average declared flops, average host speed, average site CPU utilization at launch, average grid CPU utilization at launch, and count of distinct sites active — reconstructing the grid workload arrival profile over simulated time and tracking how CPU pressure across both local and global layers evolves as job submissions accumulate?
sql: SELECT CAST(TIME / 3600 AS INTEGER) AS sim_hour, COUNT(*) AS jobs_started, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cores') AS INTEGER)) AS avg_cores, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS REAL)) AS avg_flops, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.speed') AS REAL)) AS avg_host_speed, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_site_cpu_util_at_start, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_grid_cpu_util_at_start, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site')) AS distinct_sites_active FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Started' GROUP BY sim_hour ORDER BY sim_hour

question: For the 10 file names with the highest average link load across finished FileTransfer events, what is the transfer count, average link load, maximum link load, average file size in bytes, average effective throughput in bytes per second (size divided by duration), and count of distinct source-to-destination routes used — pinpointing which specific files are consistently routed across the most congested network paths and whether that congestion is driven by file bulk, transfer frequency, or broad route diversity?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.file') AS file_name, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load') AS REAL)) AS avg_link_load, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load') AS REAL)) AS max_link_load, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS avg_file_size_bytes, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_effective_throughput_bytes_per_sec, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.source_site') || '->' || json_extract(METADATA, '$.destination_site')) AS distinct_route_count FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY file_name ORDER BY avg_link_load DESC LIMIT 10

question: For finished FileRead events grouped by disk identifier and site, what is the read operation count, count of distinct jobs that accessed the disk, count of distinct files read, total bytes read, average actual read throughput in bytes per second (size divided by duration), maximum actual read throughput, and average read duration in seconds — identifying which individual physical disks carry the heaviest read workload and whether high-volume disks experience consistent or degraded throughput performance?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk') AS disk, json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS read_count, COUNT(DISTINCT JOB_ID) AS distinct_job_count, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.file')) AS distinct_file_count, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS total_bytes_read, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_read_throughput_bps, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS max_read_throughput_bps, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration_sec FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileRead' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY disk, site ORDER BY total_bytes_read DESC

question: For finished FileWrite events grouped by integer simulation-time hour bucket (floor of TIME divided by 3600), what is the write count, count of distinct sites writing, total bytes written, average actual write throughput in bytes per second (size divided by duration), average site storage utilization, average grid storage utilization, and count of writes completed when site storage utilization exceeded 0.8 — tracking how disk output volume, write performance, and storage saturation pressure evolve across the simulation timeline?
sql: SELECT CAST(TIME / 3600 AS INTEGER) AS hour_bucket, COUNT(*) AS write_count, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site')) AS distinct_sites, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS total_bytes_written, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_write_throughput_bps, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_site_storage_util, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_grid_storage_util, SUM(CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_storage_util') AS REAL) > 0.8 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS high_storage_util_count FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileWrite' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY hour_bucket ORDER BY hour_bucket

question: For finished JobExecution events grouped by job status at completion, what is the job count, average cost, average execution duration in seconds, average retry count, maximum retry count, average total queue time in seconds, and average grid CPU utilization at completion — revealing how each recorded job outcome maps to resource consumption, scheduling overhead, failure rate, and cost across the entire simulation?
sql: SELECT STATUS, COUNT(*) AS job_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL)) AS avg_cost, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration_sec, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS REAL)) AS avg_retries, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS INTEGER)) AS max_retries, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_queue_time') AS REAL)) AS avg_total_queue_time_sec, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_grid_cpu_util FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY STATUS ORDER BY job_count DESC

question: For the 10 hosts with the highest job count among finished JobExecution events grouped by host and site, what is the job count, count of jobs that required at least one retry, average cost, average execution duration in seconds, average actual compute rate in flops per second (flops divided by duration), average site CPU utilization, and average grid CPU utilization at completion — identifying which individual compute hosts process the most work and revealing their computational throughput efficiency and failure rates?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.host') AS host, json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS job_count, SUM(CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS INTEGER) > 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS retried_job_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL)) AS avg_cost, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration_sec, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_compute_rate_flops_per_sec, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_site_cpu_util, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_grid_cpu_util FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY host, site ORDER BY job_count DESC LIMIT 10

question: For finished FileTransfer events grouped by source site, what is the transfer count, average declared bandwidth in bytes per second, average effective throughput in bytes per second (size divided by duration), average bandwidth utilization ratio (effective throughput divided by declared bandwidth), minimum bandwidth utilization ratio, and count of transfers where effective throughput fell below half the declared bandwidth — exposing which sites' outbound network links most chronically underdeliver their rated capacity and where data export efficiency is most severely degraded?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.source_site') AS source_site, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.bandwidth') AS REAL)) AS avg_declared_bandwidth_bps, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_effective_throughput_bps, AVG((CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.bandwidth') AS REAL)) AS avg_bandwidth_utilization_ratio, MIN((CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.bandwidth') AS REAL)) AS min_bandwidth_utilization_ratio, SUM(CASE WHEN (CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) < 0.5 * CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.bandwidth') AS REAL) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS below_half_bandwidth_count FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY source_site ORDER BY avg_bandwidth_utilization_ratio ASC

question: For finished JobExecution events grouped by site, what is the job count, average file-transfer queue time in seconds, average resource-waiting queue time in seconds, average total queue time in seconds, average share of total queue time attributable to file-transfer waiting (file_transfer_queue_time divided by total_queue_time), and count of jobs where file-transfer queue time strictly exceeded resource-waiting queue time — decomposing scheduling latency at each site into data-staging delays versus compute-resource contention to reveal which bottleneck dominates?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS job_count, AVG(json_extract(METADATA, '$.file_transfer_queue_time')) AS avg_file_transfer_queue_time, AVG(json_extract(METADATA, '$.resource_waiting_queue_time')) AS avg_resource_waiting_queue_time, AVG(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_queue_time')) AS avg_total_queue_time, AVG(CASE WHEN json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_queue_time') > 0 THEN json_extract(METADATA, '$.file_transfer_queue_time') / json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_queue_time') ELSE NULL END) AS avg_file_transfer_share, SUM(CASE WHEN json_extract(METADATA, '$.file_transfer_queue_time') > json_extract(METADATA, '$.resource_waiting_queue_time') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS jobs_where_file_transfer_dominated FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site ORDER BY avg_file_transfer_share DESC

question: For the 10 file names with the highest read count among finished FileRead events grouped by file name, what is the read count, count of distinct sites that accessed the file, count of distinct jobs that triggered reads of the file, total bytes read, average read duration in seconds, and average actual read throughput in bytes per second (size divided by duration) — identifying which individual input files serve as the most widely shared data hot-spots and whether high-demand files suffer degraded read throughput due to contention?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.file') AS file_name, COUNT(*) AS read_count, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site')) AS distinct_sites, COUNT(DISTINCT JOB_ID) AS distinct_jobs, SUM(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size')) AS total_bytes_read, AVG(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration')) AS avg_read_duration, AVG(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') / json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration')) AS avg_actual_read_throughput FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileRead' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY file_name ORDER BY read_count DESC LIMIT 10

question: For finished FileTransfer events grouped by source_site and destination_site pair, what is the transfer count, average transfer duration in seconds, average file size in bytes, total bytes transferred, average link load, average latency, and average effective throughput in bytes per second (size divided by duration) — mapping the busiest and most congested inter-site network routes in the simulation and revealing whether high-volume routes achieve efficient or degraded delivery?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.source_site') AS source_site, json_extract(METADATA, '$.destination_site') AS destination_site, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count, AVG(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration')) AS avg_duration, AVG(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size')) AS avg_file_size, SUM(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size')) AS total_bytes_transferred, AVG(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load')) AS avg_link_load, AVG(json_extract(METADATA, '$.latency')) AS avg_latency, AVG(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') / json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration')) AS avg_effective_throughput FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY source_site, destination_site ORDER BY transfer_count DESC

question: For finished FileWrite events grouped by disk identifier and site, what is the write count, count of distinct jobs that wrote to the disk, total bytes written, average declared disk write bandwidth in bytes per second, average actual write throughput in bytes per second (size divided by duration), average write efficiency ratio (actual throughput divided by declared bandwidth), and count of writes where actual throughput fell below half the declared bandwidth — identifying which physical disks carry the heaviest output workload and measuring how closely each disk meets its rated write capacity under simulation load?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk') AS disk, json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS write_count, COUNT(DISTINCT JOB_ID) AS distinct_jobs, SUM(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size')) AS total_bytes_written, AVG(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_write_bw')) AS avg_declared_write_bw, AVG(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') / json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration')) AS avg_actual_throughput, AVG((json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') / json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration')) / json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_write_bw')) AS avg_write_efficiency, SUM(CASE WHEN (json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') / json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration')) < 0.5 * json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_write_bw') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS writes_below_half_declared_bw FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileWrite' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY disk, site ORDER BY total_bytes_written DESC

question: For finished FileTransfer events grouped by integer simulation-time hour bucket (floor of TIME divided by 3600), what is the transfer count, total bytes transferred, average effective throughput in bytes per second (size divided by duration), average link load, maximum link load, average grid storage utilization, and count of distinct source sites active — tracking how inter-site data movement volume, network congestion, and grid-wide storage pressure co-evolve across the simulation timeline to identify peak transfer periods?
sql: SELECT CAST(TIME / 3600 AS INTEGER) AS hour_bucket, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count, SUM(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size')) AS total_bytes_transferred, AVG(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') / json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration')) AS avg_effective_throughput, AVG(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load')) AS avg_link_load, MAX(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load')) AS max_link_load, AVG(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_storage_util')) AS avg_grid_storage_util, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.source_site')) AS distinct_source_sites FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY hour_bucket ORDER BY hour_bucket
